en: 47 (143). O Maker of the material world, thou Holy One ! How far from the fire ? How far from ^ Corniption being worse in summer.
months (9 times 30 days), generally extends along ten months on the calendar (for instance from January ro to October 10).
FARGARD V. 63 the water ? How far from the consecrated bundles of Baresma ? How far from the faithful ?
48 (144). Ahura Mazda answered : * Thirty paces from the fire ; thirty paces from the water ; thirty paces from the consecrated bundles of Baresma; three paces from the faithful ^ ; —
49 (^45)- * O" ^^^^ place shall the worshippers of Mazda erect an enclosure ^ and therein shall they establish her with food, therein shall they establish her with clothes.'
50 (147). O Maker of the material world, thou Holy One ! What is the food that the woman shall first take ?

51(148). Ahura Mazda answered: 'G6m6z^ mixed with ashes, three draughts of it, or six, or nine, to send down the Dakhma within her womb \ 52(151). 'Afterwards she may drink boiling* milk of mares, cows, sheep, or goats, with pap or widiout pap^; she may take cooked milk widiout ^ The earner alone is kept thirty feet from the faithful (Farg. Ill, 18), as he is cut off from the community : bis food is not brought to him, he has a store prepared for him. The woman, when arm6xt, is only temporarily isolated; she stays in the house and her food is brought to her all but from hand to hand (Farg. XVI, 6).
• The place for the man or woman in state of uncleanness, or Armfixt-gdh.
• Urine of the ox:, the so-called Ntrajig-din; cf. Farg. VIII, 37; XIX, 21. 'Three cups, or six, or nine, according to her strength ' (Asp.)

^ Her womb is a Dakhma, as it contained, a dead body. — These nine draughts of gdmfes mixed with ashes are like an interior Barashnftm, as the Barashnftm consists of nine successive purifica^ tions with gdm£z and dust.
• Doubtful. • Doubtful.
64 VENDtoAD.
water, meal without water, and wine without water \* 53 (154). O Maker of the material world, thou Holy One ! How long shall she remain so ? How long shall she live thus on milk, meal, and wine ?

54 (^55)' Ahura Mazda answered : ' Three nights long shall she remain so ; three nights long shall she live thus on milk, meal, and wine. Then, when three nights have passed, she shall wash her body, she shall wash her clothes, with gdm6z and water, by the nine holes ^ and thus shall she be clean/ 55 (157)- O Maker of the material world, thou Holy One ! How long shall she remain so ? How long, after the three nights have gone, shall she sit confined, and live separated from the rest of the worshippers of Mazda, as to her seat, her food, and her clothing ?

56 (158). Ahura Mazda answered : ' Nine nights long shall she remain so : nine nights long, after the three nights have gone, shall she sit confined, and live separated from the rest of the worshippers of Mazda, as to her seat, her food, and her clothing. Then, when the nine nights have gone, she shall wash her body, and cleanse her clothes with gdmdz and water'.* ^ * The water would be defiled; ' cf. Farg. VII, 70 seq.
details of which see Farg. IX. That Barashnfim is taken forty days after the delivery.

' ' If a woman brings forth a still-bom child, ader a pregnancy of one month to ten months, the first food she shall take is ntrang (=gdm6z) ... fire and ashes; and she is not allowed until the fourth day to take water or salt, or any food that is cooked with water or salt: on the fourth day they give her nlrang, that she may cleanse herself and wash her clothes with it, and she is not FARGARD V. 65 57 (160) *• O Maker of the material world, thou Holy One ! Can those clothes, when once washed and cleansed, ever be used either by a Zaotar, or by a H&vanan, or by an Atare-vakhsha, or by a Frabaretar, or by an Ahered, or by an Asnatar, or by a Rathwi^kar, or by a Sraoshi-varez *, or by any priest, warrior, or husbandman^?

58(162). Ahura Mazda answered: 'Never can those clothes, even when washed and cleansed, be used either by a Zaotar, or by a Hivanan, or by an Atare-vakhsha, or by a Frabaretar, or by an Ahtrtd, or by an Asnatar, or by a Rathwi5kan or by a Sraosh^-varez, or by any priest, warrior, or husbandman.

59 (164). 'But if there be in a Mazdean house a woman who is in her sickness, or a man who has become unfit for works and who must sit in the place of infirmity S those clothes shall serve for their allowed to wash herself and her clothes with water until the fortyfirst day ' (Gr. Rav. 568).

in the sacrifices. The Hflvanan strains the Haoma; the Atarevakhsha kindles the fire : the Frabaretar brings to the Zaotar all that he needs; the Abere^ brings the water; the Asnatar washes and strains the Haoma; the Rathwirkar mixes the Haoma and the milk ; the 2^otar chants the hjrmns and says the prayers ; the Sraoshft-vares superintends the sacrifice. Nowadays there are only two priesU, the 2^tar (Zfttt) and the Rathwirkar (Raspt), the htter performing all the accessory services formerly performed by several priests. Cf. Nhang^n, {§ 7 < sq- who is unclean, during the time of his uncleanness (Farg. IX, 33 s^0> ^^^ ^^^ ^^^^ ^ forbidden to him.
[4] F 66 VENDtoAD.

coverings and for their sheets \ until they can withdraw their hands for prayer \ 60 (168). * Ahura Mazda, indeed, does not allow us to waste anything of value that we may have, not even so much as an Asperena's ' weight of thread, not even so much as a maid lets fall in spinning.
61 (171). 'Whosoever throws any clothing on a dead body*, even so much as a maid lets fall in spinning, is not a pious man whilst alive, nor shall he, when dead, have a place in Paradise.

62 (174). * He makes himself a viaticum unto the world of the wicked, into that world *, made of ^ The clothing defiled by the dead can only serve for Dashtin women, even after it has been washed and exposed for six months to the light of the sun and of the moon (Saddar 91 ; of. Farg. VII, 10 seq.)
wrapped in an old piece of linen, lest they should touch and defile anything clean.
' See Farg. IV, 48, note 4.

the dead must lie on the mountain naked, or * clotl^sd only with the light of heaven' (Farg. VI, 51). The modem custom is to clothe them with old clothing (Dadabhai Naoroji, Manners and Customs of the Parsis, p. 15). 'When a man dies and receives the order (to depart), the older the shroud they make for him, the better. It must be old, worn out, but well washed : they must not lay anything new on the dead. For it is said in the Zend Vendidid, ^f they put on the dead even so much as a thread from the distafif more than is necessary, every thread shall become in the other world a black snake clinging to the heart of him who made that shroud, and even the dead shall rise against him and seize him by the skirt, and say. That shroud which thou madest for me has become food for worms and vermin ' (Saddar za). After the fourth day, when the soul is in heaven, then rich garments are offered up to it, which it will wear in its celestial life (Saddar 87).
Aogomaid6 28); something more than the 'visible darkness.'
FARGARD VI. 67 darkness, the offspring of darkness^, which is Darkness self. To that world, to the world of Hell, you are delivered by your own doings, by your own religion, O sinners^!'
Fargard VI.
zh: 啊，物質世界的造物者，至聖者！距離火焰何其遠？距離^腐敗在夏季更為惡劣之處何其遠？
個月（9次30天），通常在日曆上延續十個月（例如從1月至10月10日）。
FARGARD V. 63 那水？距離聖別的巴雷斯瑪（Baresma）束有多遠？距離信徒有多遠？
阿胡拉‧瑪茲達回答：「距離火三十步；距離水三十步；距離聖化的巴雷斯瑪（Baresma）束三十步；距離信徒三步；——
瑪茲達（Mazda）的信徒們應在此地豎立一道圍欄，並在其中以食物供奉她，在其中以衣物供奉她。
50（147）。物質世界的創造者啊，聖者啊！女人首先應當食用什麼食物呢？

51(148)。阿胡拉‧馬茲達回答說：「蒙茲（Gômez）混合灰燼，她應當飲用三口、六口或九口，以將其送入她的達赫瑪（Dakhma）之內的子宮¹。52(151)。「之後，她可以飲用煮沸的母馬、母牛、綿羊或山羊的牛奶，可帶粥或不帶粥²；她可以取用煮過的牛奶而無³。污穢者單獨被隔離在信徒外三十英尺處（Farg. III, 18），因為他被逐出社群：他的食物不被帶給他，而是為他準備一份儲備。婦女在污穢時，只是暫時被隔離；她留在家中，她的食物被帶給她，但幾乎不是直接遞交（Farg. XVI, 6）。
• 不潔淨狀態之男性或女性的位置，或稱 Armfixt-gdh。
• 牛尿：所謂的 Ntrajig-din；參見《法爾瓦定經》（Farg.）VIII, 37；XIX, 21。「三杯、六杯或九杯，取決於她的體力」（《阿斯潘德經》Asp.）
^ 她的子宮是一座達克瑪（Dakhma），因為它曾容納死屍。——這九次摻有灰燼的聖火祭祀所飲之物就像內在的巴拉什努姆（Barashnftm），因為巴拉什努姆由九次連續的聖火祭祀與塵埃的淨化儀式所組成。
• 可疑。• 可疑。
我已準備好進行翻譯，但我發現您提供的英文原文為「64 VENDtoAD.」，這似乎不是完整的文本內容，可能是掃描或轉錄時出現的錯誤。 請問您能否提供： 1. 完整的英文原文段落？ 2. 或確認這是否為頁碼標註而非實際翻譯內容？ 待您提供完整的英文原文後，我將按照您的規則進行精確的繁體中文翻譯。
水、無水的麥粉，以及無水的葡萄酒 * 53 (154)。啊，物質世界的造物主，至聖者！她將如此存在多久？她將如此靠著牛奶、麥粉和葡萄酒而活多久？
54 (^55)「阿胡拉‧馬茲達答道：『三個夜晚她將保持如此狀態；三個夜晚她將以乳、麥粉和酒如此生活。然後，當三個夜晚過去後，她應以 gdm6z 和水洗淨她的身體，她應以 gdm6z 和水洗淨她的衣服，以九孔而洗淨^ 如此她便潔淨矣。』55 (157)『物質世界的創造者啊，聖者啊！她將保持如此狀態多久？三個夜晚過去後，她應隔離坐著多久，並與馬茲達的其他信徒在座位、食物和衣著上分離而生活？
阿胡拉‧瑪茲達回答道：「她當如此守持九個夜晚；在那三個夜晚過去之後，她將在九個夜晚的期間內保持禁閉，在座位、食物和衣著方面與其餘的瑪茲達信徒分離而居。然後，當那九個夜晚過去時，她應當洗淨其身體，並用 gdmdz 和水潔淨其衣物。」* * 水會被污穢；參閱 *Farg.* VII, 70 seq.
詳見《法爾剛經》第九卷。芭剌什尼姆（Barashnfim）係在分娩後第四十天時進行。

「如果一個婦女在懷孕一個月至十個月後生下一個死胎，她最初應食用的食物是 ntrang（=gdm6z）……火與灰燼；在第四天之前，她不被允許服用水或鹽，或任何用水或鹽烹製的食物：到了第四天，他們給她 nlrang，使她能夠藉此潔淨自己並洗淨其衣物，她不被…… **FARGARD V. 65** 物質世界的造物主，聖聖者啊！這些衣物一旦洗淨和潔淨後，可曾被用於 Zaotar、或 Havanan、或 Atare-vakhsha、或 Frabaretar、或 Ahered、或 Asnatar、或 Rathwikar、或 Sraoshi-varez*，或任何祭司、戰士或農夫身上？」
阿胡拉‧馬茲達回答道：『即便衣物被洗淨與潔淨，也絕不能被查祖塔（Zaotar）、希瓦南（Hivanan）、阿塔瑞‧瓦克沙（Atare-vakhsha）、夫拉巴瑞塔（Frabaretar）、阿赫特德（Ahtrtd）、阿斯納塔（Asnatar）、拉瑟維肯（Rathwikar）或斯拉奧什‧瓦瑞茲（Sraosh-varez）所使用，也不能被任何祭司、戰士或農夫所使用。』
59 (164). 「但如果在祆教之家有一位患病中的婦女，或一位已經不適合工作而必須坐在病患之處的男子，那些衣服應當供他們使用；直到第四十一日，她才被允許用水洗淨自己和她的衣服」（希臘文 Rav. 568）。

在祭祀中，赫萬南（Hflvanan）濾取贊瑪（Haoma）；阿塔瑞瓦克沙（Atarevakhsha）點燃聖火；弗拉巴瑞塔（Frabaretar）將祭司所需的一切帶給扎歐塔（Zaotar）；阿貝瑞（Abere）帶來聖水；阿斯納塔（Asnater）洗滌並濾取贊瑪；拉特維爾卡（Rathwirkar）混合贊瑪與乳汁；佐塔（Zotar）誦唱讚歌並誦念禱詞；斯拉歐什塔－瓦瑞斯（Sraoshâ-vares）監督祭祀。現今只有兩位祭司，即佐塔（Zott）和拉特維爾卡（Raspt），後者執行了先前由多位祭司分別執行的所有輔助性儀式。參見《涅昂古經》（Nhang'n），§ 7 及以下；凡不潔者（Farg. IX, 33）在其不潔期間，此等工作即被禁止他進行。
我注意到您提供的「英文原文」只包含： [4] F 66 VENDtoAD. 這似乎是檔案編號或掃描標記，而非穆勒《宗教學導論》（1873）的實際文本內容。 請提供完整的英文原文段落，我便能為您提供準確的繁體中文翻譯。
用來蓋覆和作為床單，直到他們能夠抽出雙手進行祈禱為止 (168)。* 阿胡拉‧馬茲達確實不允許我們浪費任何有價值的東西，即使我們擁有，也不容許浪費一根阿斯佩雷納的重量那麼多的線絲，甚至不容許女僕在紡織時掉落的線絲。
61 (171). 「凡人若在死者身上拋擲任何衣物，即使只是女僕紡織時掉落的布料，其人活著時就不是虔誠之人，死後亦不得進入天堂。」
62（174）。* 他為自己製造了一個通行證進入惡人的世界，進入那個世界 *，由 ^ 被死人玷污的衣物只能用於達什廷婦女，即使經過洗滌並在日月光下暴曬六個月之後也是如此（《薩達爾》91；參見《法爾加德》第七卷，第十節及之後）
用一塊舊亞麻布包裹著，以免它們觸碰並玷污任何潔淨的東西。
見Farg. IV, 48，注4。

死者必須裸露地躺在山上，或*僅以天堂之光覆蓋之*（Farg. VI, 51）。現代習俗則是用舊衣物為亡者裹身（Dadabhai Naoroji, Manners and Customs of the Parsis, p. 15）。「當人死亡並受命（離去）時，為其製作的裹屍布愈古老，愈為妥善。此布必須是陳舊、磨損的，但應充分洗淨：不可在死者身上放置任何新物。蓋《堅德‧萬迪達德》(Zend Vendidid)有云，倘若於死者身上放置即使只是來自紡錘的一根線，超過必要之量，則每根線在來世都將化為黑蛇，纏繞於製作裹屍布者之心，連亡者本身亦將起身反抗他，揪住其衣角，並言：『汝為我製作的這條裹屍布已成為蟲蛆之食物』」（Saddar za）。過了第四日，當靈魂升入天堂後，則將富裕的衣著獻給它，它將在其天界生命中穿著（Saddar 87）。
某種超越於「可見之黑暗」(Aogomaid6 28)的東西。
法爾加德第六篇 67 黑暗，黑暗之子^，即黑暗本身。至彼世界，至地獄之世界，爾等罪人乃由爾等自身之行為、爾等自身之宗教而被遣往彼處^！
